qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unsafe pickle deserialization permits attacker-controlled code execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes unsafe pickle deserialization (pickle.load/pickle.loads) in grammar.py and cache.py leading to arbitrary code execution. The advisory describes a completely different root cause: unsanitized --python-cell-magics option value used in cache filename construction enabling path traversal and arbitrary file writes (CWE-22). The vulnerable code paths, root-cause mechanisms, and security consequences are distinct. The finding involves deserialization of attacker-controlled pickle objects, while the advisory involves missing input sanitization in filename generation. Both involve Black's cache system but at different layers with different root causes.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding matches the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory (GHSA-3936-cmfr-pm3m) describes a path traversal vulnerability in Black's cache filename construction where the --python-cell-magics option value is placed in the filename without sanitization, allowing arbitrary file writes (CWE-22). The sole qualifying finding describes unsafe pickle deserialization in grammar.py and cache.py (pickle.load/pickle.loads without validation), which is a different vulnerability class, different root cause, different vulnerable code path, and different security consequence (arbitrary code execution vs arbitrary file write).
evidence: Advisory root cause: unsanitized --python-cell-magics value in cache filename construction
Finding root cause: pickle.load()/pickle.loads() without validation in grammar.py and cache.py
Advisory CWE: CWE-22 (Path Traversal), Finding type: deserialization_rce
Advisory impact: arbitrary file writes; Finding impact: arbitrary code execution
Different vulnerable code: advisory affects filename computation logic, finding affects deserialization calls at src/blib2to3/pgen2/grammar.py:124,129 and src/black/cache.py:80
